subject: Constitutional Law
module: Articles 41, 42 and 42A — Family, Education and Children

lessons.title: The Family — Article 41
lessons.description: Article 41 recognises the Family as the natural primary and fundamental unit group of Society — a moral institution with inalienable and imprescriptible rights antecedent and superior to all positive law. That protection was historically confined to the marital family, as confirmed in The State (Nicolaou) v An Bord Uchtála, but has been progressively challenged through referendum, litigation, and — most recently — the contested 2024 referendums on the definition of family and care.

lessons.title: Children's Rights — Article 42A
